message:
chore(deps): bump franken-agent-detection + collapse asupersync stack

Two coordinated dependency moves:

1. franken-agent-detection git rev 88756ba9 → 5b8c4a27ce7a503b8c63fb
   383e1b99ce8364f63a. Picks up upstream's asupersync 0.3.x alignment
   so it no longer drags a parallel asupersync 0.2.9 copy into the
   lockfile.

2. Lockfile cleanup. The old Cargo.toml carried a long comment
   cataloguing the two transitive asupersync 0.2.9 sources
   (frankensqlite's registry copy at 0.2.9 and frankensearch's git
   rev 08dd31df pinned at 0.2.9). Those collapsed once:
     - frankensqlite cut crates.io release with 0.3.x-aligned deps
     - our frankensearch dep rev 3dbab624 (current) consumes 0.3.x
     - franken-agent-detection rev 5b8c4a27 (this commit) consumes 0.3.x
   Rewrote the comment to reflect the resolved state: "pin the three
   git revs to keep 0.3.x; otherwise a downgrade reintroduces the 0.2.x
   parallel tree". Cargo.lock shrinks by ~500 net lines from removing
   the asupersync 0.2.9 branch (plus its transitive duplicates).

The local-checkout override instructions in the asupersync comment
block stay intact — unchanged workflow for runtime hackers.

-# Asupersync version split — PARTIALLY resolved. The direct cass dep
-# is now on crates.io asupersync 0.3.0 (no more git rev pin), but the
-# lockfile still carries two older asupersync 0.2.9 copies pulled in
-# transitively:
-#   - registry copy via frankensqlite (crates.io frankensqlite still
-#     depends on asupersync 0.2.9)
-#   - git rev 08dd31df via frankensearch (this project's frankensearch
-#     dep uses rev db7530e5, which still references asupersync 0.2.9)
-# Both collapse into a single asupersync 0.3.0 entry once frankensqlite
-# and frankensearch cut matching 0.3-era releases on crates.io and we
-# bump the deps here to consume them.
+# The dependency graph is pinned to the asupersync 0.3.x line. Keep
+# frankensqlite, frankensearch, and franken-agent-detection revs aligned
+# so Cargo.lock does not reintroduce a parallel asupersync 0.2.x stack.
 #
 # If you need to build against a local checkout of asupersync (sibling
 # at ../asupersync) during runtime-level hacking, add the following
